messages.content: Which model is best for coding right now, and how confident should I be?

---

## Best model for coding right now: **Claude Opus 4.8**

### The short answer
**Claude Opus 4.8** is the current leader on the most credible independent coding benchmark. But the gap from #2 is real, the benchmark is contaminated, and the *best* model by some measures (Claude Mythos) isn't publicly available yet.

---

### The evidence

| Rank | Model | SWE-bench Verified (vals.ai) | Cost/test | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **Claude Opus 4.8** | **88.60% ±1.42** | $1.92 | ✅ Independent, updated 2026-06-02 |
| 2 | GPT 5.5 | 82.60% ±1.70 | $1.36 | ✅ Independent |
| 3 | Claude Opus 4.7 | 82.00% ±1.72 | $2.42 | ✅ Independent |
| 4 | Gemini 3.5 Flash | 78.80% ±1.83 | $0.95 | ✅ Independent; best speed |
| 4 | Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview | 78.80% ±1.83 | $0.78 | ✅ Independent; cheapest top-5 |
| — | **Claude Mythos Preview** | **93.9%** (BenchLM.ai) | N/A | ⚠️ Not publicly available |

Source: [vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench](https://vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench), leaderboard updated **2026-06-02**.

**Why the Opus 4.8 lead is real:** The gap over GPT 5.5 (88.6% vs 82.6%) is ~6 points, well outside both models' error bars (±1.4 and ±1.7). It's especially dominant on harder tasks: 74% on 1–4 hour problems vs GPT 5.5's 50%.

---

### How confident should you be? Medium-high, with caveats.

**Reasons to trust it:**
- vals.ai uses a **standardized bash-only harness** (mini-swe-agent) applied identically to every model — this is the most apples-to-apples comparison available
- The gap is large enough to be meaningful, not noise
- The result is corroborated: Anthropic also self-reports leading SWE-bench Pro by >10 pts over GPT-5.5

**Reasons for caution:**
1. **SWE-bench Verified is contaminated.** OpenAI's own audit (2026-02-23) found ~59% of hard tasks have flawed test cases, and all frontier models show signs of training-data contamination — some can reproduce verbatim gold patches. Treat all scores as **upper bounds on real-world capability**, not clean measures.
2. **The best model isn't available to you.** Claude Mythos Preview reportedly scores 93.9% on this benchmark, but it's restricted to ~200 vetted cybersecurity orgs under Project Glasswing. Public release is "coming in weeks" per Anthropic (as of late May 2026).
3. **Benchmark ≠ your use case.** SWE-bench measures fixing Python GitHub issues. If you're doing JS, Rust, greenfield code, code review, or architecture, the ordering may differ.
4. **The field moves fast.** This lead could evaporate within weeks — Mythos public release and any OpenAI response could reshuffle rankings.

---

### Practical picks

| Use case | Pick | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Best available, cost flexible | **Claude Opus 4.8** ($5/$25/1M) | #1 independent score, especially good on hard tasks |
| Best speed/cost balance | **Gemini 3.5 Flash** ($1.50/$9/1M) | ~79% score at a fraction of the price, fastest inference |
| Budget open-weights | **DeepSeek V4** (77.4%, ~$0.44/test) | Competitive with top-tier closed models at open-weights pricing |

---

**Scorecard updated:** `/memory/scorecard.md` — Coding/agentic category reranked with Claude Opus 4.8 at #1 (88.60%, vals.ai 2026-06-02). Added Claude Mythos Preview as an unverified "#0" entry. Changelog updated 2026-06-04. `/memory/AGENTS.md` — Opus 4.8 verified item marked complete.
What are the most important LLM benchmarks, and can I trust them?

My memory already has deep, current knowledge on this. Here's a comprehensive, sourced answer:

---

## The Most Important LLM Benchmarks — and How Much to Trust Them

**Short answer:** Benchmark scores are claims, not facts. The best benchmarks are independent, contamination-resistant, and unsaturated. Most headline numbers you see in press releases aren't those. Here's the map:

---

### The Major Benchmarks by Category

#### 🧠 General Knowledge / Reasoning
| Benchmark | What it tests | Trust level |
|---|---|---|
| **MMLU** | ~57 academic subjects, multiple choice | ⚠️ Low — heavily contaminated (public for years), largely saturated at frontier tier. Don't rank on it. |
| **MMLU-Pro** | Harder 10-choice variant | Better, but still public and aging fast. |
| **GPQA Diamond** | PhD-level science (biology, chemistry, physics) | ✅ Good — hard enough to avoid saturation, less contaminated. Current frontier scores: ~80–90%. |
| **HLE** ("Humanity's Last Exam") | Extremely hard expert questions | ✅ Best for discrimination at frontier — still unsaturated. Very new. |
| **BIG-Bench Hard** | Diverse challenging tasks | Reasonable but showing age; frontier models are approaching saturation on subsets. |

#### 🔢 Math
| Benchmark | Trust level |
|---|---|
| **GSM8K** | ❌ Saturated. Top models score ~99%. Useless for ranking frontier models. |
| **MATH** | ⚠️ Partially saturated at the top end. Better than GSM8K but watch for contamination. |
| **AIME (fresh years)** | ✅ Best math benchmark right now — time-windowed, competition problems, hard enough to differentiate. Use the *current year* AIME, not archived. |

#### 💻 Coding / Agentic
| Benchmark | Trust level |
|---|---|
| **SWE-bench Verified** | ⚠️ **Contaminated as of 2026-02-23** — OpenAI's own audit found ~59% of hard tasks have flawed test cases, and frontier models can reproduce verbatim gold patches. OpenAI stopped reporting it. Treat as upper bound. Source: [openai.com/index/why-we-no-longer-evaluate-swe-bench-verified/](https://openai.com/index/why-we-no-longer-evaluate-swe-bench-verified/) |
| **SWE-bench Pro** | ✅ The recommended replacement (Scale AI, 1,865 tasks, 5 languages). Harder, newer, less contaminated. Still maturing. |
| **LiveCodeBench** | ✅ Good — time-windowed (new problems continuously added), more contamination-resistant than HumanEval. |
| **vals.ai harness (mini-swe-agent)** | ✅ Independent, standardized bash-only harness for fair cross-model comparison on SWE-bench Verified. Best independent coding source right now. |
| **HumanEval / MBPP** | ❌ Saturated and heavily contaminated. Don't use for frontier comparisons. |
| **Terminal-Bench** | Newer; measures agentic shell/terminal tasks. Less established but contamination-resistant. |

#### 🤖 Agentic / Tool Use
| Benchmark | Notes |
|---|---|
| **GAIA** | Tests real-world tool-use and multi-step reasoning. Reasonable but still maturing. |
| **tau-bench** | Tool-calling, more realistic than synthetic suites. Small but valid. |
| **WebArena / Mind2Web** | Web navigation and browser-use agents. Construct-valid but hard to standardize. |

#### 📄 Long Context
| Benchmark | Notes |
|---|---|
| **RULER** | Synthetic retrieval and comprehension over long docs. Independent, reasonable. |
| **Needle-in-a-haystack** | Simple retrieval test — good for floor/ceiling testing but not nuanced. |
| **LongBench** | Diverse real-world long-context tasks. More construct-valid than NIAH. |

#### 👥 Human Preference
| Benchmark | Trust level |
|---|---|
| **LMArena (Chatbot Arena) Elo** | ✅ Best for "what do people prefer" — large-scale blind human comparisons. **Caveat:** measures preference, not correctness. Close Elo gaps (< ~10 pts) are within confidence intervals — don't call them wins. Source: [arena.ai/leaderboard/text](https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text) |

#### 📊 Aggregators
| Tool | Notes |
|---|---|
| **Artificial Analysis** | Blends quality, speed, and price into a single index. Useful for a quick orientation. Not a substitute for task-specific evaluation. |
| **BenchLM.ai** | Newer aggregator; methodology not fully verified — treat with caution. |

---

### The Validity Checklist — Run Every Score Through This

Before trusting any benchmark claim, ask:

1. **Independent or self-reported?** Self-reported = lab ran it themselves, often with best-of-N, custom prompts, or scaffold help. Treat as an *upper bound*, not a fair comparison.
2. **Contamination?** Could the test data be in the training set? Public benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K) almost certainly are. Prefer time-windowed or held-out sets.
3. **Saturated?** If the top-10 models all score >90%, the benchmark can't discriminate. Small gaps are noise.
4. **Same harness?** Scores swing wildly with prompt format, few-shot count, chain-of-thought, and scaffolding. Never compare scores run on different harnesses.
5. **Construct validity?** Does the benchmark actually test what matters to you? Multiple-choice MMLU ≠ real reasoning. Isolated function coding ≠ working in a large codebase.
6. **Statistical significance?** Check confidence intervals. A 2% gap on N=500 tasks is often not significant.
7. **Gaming?** Watch for models trained specifically on a popular benchmark. Cross-check against held-out or human evals.
8. **Date / model version?** Leaderboards lag. A score from 6 months ago may reflect an outdated model or a since-contaminated benchmark.

---

### One-Line Summary by Use Case

| You want to know… | Best source |
|---|---|
| Which model people *prefer* for chat | LMArena Elo (with CI caveat) |
| Which model is best at *coding agents* | vals.ai SWE-bench Verified / SWE-bench Pro |
| Which model is best at *hard reasoning/science* | GPQA Diamond (independent), HLE, fresh AIME |
| Best model for your *price point* | Artificial Analysis quality-per-dollar index |
| Which model the *lab claims* is best | Press release / model card — treat as upper bound |

---

### Current State of Trust (as of June 2026)

- **Most trustworthy right now:** vals.ai coding leaderboard (independent, standardized), LMArena (large N, blind human votes), GPQA Diamond (hard, less contaminated), fresh AIME, SWE-bench Pro (newer, harder).
- **Deprecated / don't rank on:** GSM8K, HumanEval, MMLU, SWE-bench Verified (contaminated per OpenAI's own audit).
- **Healthy skepticism zone:** Any self-reported score in a launch blog post — especially on benchmarks the lab chose to highlight. Always ask: *what did they not report?*

The TL;DR: A high benchmark score is a **marketing claim until independently verified on a contamination-resistant, unsaturated benchmark**. Cross-check against at least one independent source before drawing conclusions.

---

*No scorecard updates needed — this answer draws entirely from my existing memory/skill knowledge, which is current as of 2026-06-04.*
How do Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google compare as frontier LLM labs, and what have they shipped recently?

Now the full answer:

---

## Anthropic vs. OpenAI vs. Google DeepMind — Current State (June 2026)

These three labs collectively define the frontier. Here's how they compare across dimensions that matter, grounded in what's been verified recently.

---

### 🔵 Anthropic

**Model family:** Claude — Opus (flagship), Sonnet (mid-tier), Haiku (fast/cheap)

**Current top model:** **Claude Opus 4.8** (released May 28, 2026)
- **SWE-bench Verified: 88.60% ±1.42** ([vals.ai](https://vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench), June 2, 2026) — **#1 on the independent leaderboard**, beating GPT-5.5 by ~6 points outside the error bars
- Leads SWE-bench Pro by >10 pts over GPT-5.5 (Anthropic self-reported)
- Key new capability: **Dynamic Workflows** — coordinates swarms of 10s–100s of parallel subagents for large agentic tasks
- **4× improvement in honesty** (self-uncertainty calibration vs. Opus 4.7)
- Pricing: $5/$25 per 1M tokens in/out — same as Opus 4.7
- **LMArena**: Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7 (thinking modes) hold the **#1 and #2 overall Elo spots** (1502 and 1500); 4.8 not yet voted in

**Upcoming:** **Claude Mythos** — restricted pre-release under Project Glasswing (~200 orgs including ENISA, Amazon, Microsoft, Apple). Most anticipated near-term model. Reportedly scores **93.9% on SWE-bench Verified** per BenchLM.ai, but this is unverified. Public launch "in coming weeks" as of late May. Being withheld due to advanced cyberoffensive capabilities, not technical readiness.

**Business:** Confidentially filed for IPO (June 1, 2026). Valued at **~$965B** (largest private AI lab by valuation). Revenue ~$47B annualized. Backed by Google (~$10B committed) and Amazon (Project Rainier — $25B committed, April 2026 alone; $100B+ total over 10 years).

**Where Anthropic leads:** Coding/agentic (SWE-bench), honesty/calibration, safety-focused research, enterprise trust, human preference (LMArena top spots). Strong at long agentic tasks (15 min – 4 hr).

---

### 🟢 OpenAI

**Model family:** GPT-5.x (main line), GPT-Rosalind (specialized biodefense), o-series reasoning models

**Current top models:**
- **GPT-5.5** — SWE-bench Verified: **82.60% ±1.70** (vals.ai, independent) — #2 behind Opus 4.8. Pricing: $5/$30 per 1M tokens. 1.05M context. ARC-AGI-2 Verified: **85.0%**, Terminal-Bench 2.1: **82.7%** (strong reasoning numbers)
- **GPT-5.4** — $2.50/$15 per 1M tokens; SWE-bench 78.2%. Better cost-performance ratio
- **GPT-Rosalind** (May 29, 2026): Specialized biodefense model. Restricted to trusted public-health/gov orgs; briefed the White House. Not general purpose
- **GPT-5.5 Instant**: Fast mode recently updated; mobile app now includes effort-level picker (long-press send) and table-of-contents for long chats

**LMArena:** GPT-5.5-high ranks **#8 overall** (Elo 1482); within a competitive cluster at the top.

**Business:** Valued at **~$852B** ($122B capital commitments). Preparing IPO. $100B partnership with NVIDIA (NVIDIA gets nonvoting shares). Key backer of Stargate ($500B, 5.5 GW, 6+ sites). Also gave Japanese banks (MUFG, SMBC, Mizuho) early access to GPT-5.5 for cybersecurity.

**Where OpenAI leads:** ARC-AGI-2 reasoning, Terminal-Bench (practical tool use), breadth of consumer product (ChatGPT ~700M+ users), government partnerships, ecosystem integrations. Best-in-class reasoning benchmarks outside of coding.

---

### 🔴 Google DeepMind

**Model family:** Gemini — 3.1 Pro (flagship API), 3.5 Flash (consumer/speed), 3.5 Flash Light (lightweight), Gemini Go (edge)

**Current top models:**
- **Gemini 3.5 Flash** (announced Google I/O, May 19, 2026): SWE-bench Verified **78.80% ±1.83** (vals.ai, tied with Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview) — impressively fast ($0.95/test, 254s). Thinking modes ("Standard" and "Extended") now free for all users
- **Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview**: 78.80% SWE-bench (same tier); $2/$12 per 1M tokens; 1M context; LMArena **#6 overall** (Elo 1487)
- **Gemini Spark** (rolling out to AI Ultra subscribers, US): Persistent background agentic assistant — works on tasks without user prompting. Google's response to the agentic era
- **Gemini Go**: Replacing Google Assistant on Android Go devices (≥2GB RAM); lightweight on-device assistant

**Google I/O 2026 (May 19) themes:**
- "We are firmly in our agentic Gemini era" — Sundar Pichai
- New search overhaul: AI-packaged results (controversial)
- Android XR smart glasses: Audio glasses with Warby Parker + Gentle Monster (fall 2026), Project Aura full-display prototype shown
- Compute-based usage quotas introduced (consumer backlash; Google adjusting)

**Business:** Public (Alphabet, ~$2.5T market cap). **$180–190B capex in 2026**, raising $80B equity for AI infrastructure. TPU + GPU mixed compute strategy. Largest total distributed compute of any lab.

**Where Google leads:** Consumer/product surface area (Search, Android, Workspace, YouTube), multimodal breadth, hardware integration (TPUs, on-device Gemini), price-performance on Flash-tier models, raw infrastructure scale. Also leads on agentic consumer products (Spark).

---

### Head-to-Head Summary

| Dimension | Anthropic | OpenAI | Google |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Best coding/agentic** | ✅ Opus 4.8 (#1 SWE-bench) | #2 GPT-5.5 | #3–4 Gemini 3.5 Flash |
| **Best reasoning** | Competitive | ✅ GPT-5.5 (ARC-AGI-2 85%) | Competitive |
| **Human preference (LMArena)** | ✅ #1–4 (Opus 4.6/4.7) | #8 | #6–7 |
| **Price-performance** | Mid ($5/$25) | Mid–high ($5/$30) | ✅ Flash: $1.50/$9 |
| **Speed** | Competitive | Competitive | ✅ Gemini Flash (~fastest) |
| **Consumer ecosystem** | Claude.ai, Claude Code | ✅ ChatGPT ~700M users | ✅ Search/Android/Workspace |
| **Upcoming "big thing"** | Mythos (imminent) | Continued GPT-5.x + biodefense | Agentic Spark + XR glasses |
| **Business status** | Filing IPO; ~$965B | Filing IPO; ~$852B | Public; Alphabet ~$2.5T |

---

### Key caveats

- **SWE-bench Verified is contaminated** ([OpenAI audit, Feb 2026](https://openai.com/index/why-we-no-longer-evaluate-swe-bench-verified/)): all frontier models show signs of training-data contamination. Scores are upper bounds. SWE-bench Pro is now the recommended benchmark; Anthropic self-reports leading there too, but independent numbers are pending.
- **LMArena Elo** measures chat preference, not objective correctness. The top ~10 models are within each other's confidence intervals — treat as a cluster.
- **Claude Mythos** is the biggest wildcard. If it ships at anywhere near its reported capability, it would likely extend Anthropic's coding/agentic lead significantly.
- Google's **Gemini 3.5 Flash** punches above its price tier and is arguably the best value model for many tasks right now.

---

**What I persisted to memory:**
- `/memory/AGENTS.md`: Added Google I/O 2026 announcements (Gemini 3.5, Spark, XR glasses, agentic era theme), lab valuations and IPO filing status (Anthropic ~$965B, OpenAI ~$852B, both filing)
- Scorecard was already current with these models' benchmark scores from prior sessions — no scorecard changes needed this run
